textPlain: I given up learning any new css, keeping up to date with "solutions" that would create more problems, more code, more confusion and difficult to maintain. I feel like it's becoming this humungous monster we don't want to talk about or admit it's there, for me personally it still has all the problems we complain about since who knows when anymore

There's a point at which you start coding for the implementation, not the API. That's always dangerous.While I understand the point -- hinting to the implementation -- is it really something we need, in the sense that is CSS the right place for this, rather than handling these situations better in the browser implementation?
